_comment: Default-deny seccomp profile for ocu-filestored (T2-15, NFR-SEC-02).
_rationale: Base: tightened subset of the OCI / Moby container default allowlist.
The daemon is a static CGO_ENABLED=0 Go binary compiled with -trimpath.
It needs only: Unix-socket accept/read/write, filesystem verbs under the
engine-root/audit-sink/socket-dir mounts, Go runtime primitives, graceful
shutdown signal handling, and the NOTIFY_SOCKET sendmsg for sd_notify.
Every syscall not listed below results in ENOSYS (SCMP_ACT_ERRNO 38).
Capability group annotations show WHICH daemon need each syscall serves.
Explicitly NOT allowed (not needed — reduce attack surface):
ptrace, perf_event_open, bpf — no debugging or kernel tracing
keyctl, add_key, request_key — no kernel keyring access
mount, umount2, pivot_root — no filesystem mounting
setuid, setgid, setgroups — daemon does not change credentials at runtime
fork, vfork — daemon does not spawn child processes
execveat — only execve (own binary) is needed at startup
inotify_* — no filesystem watch needed
shmget, shmat, shmctl — no SysV shared memory
msgsnd, msgrcv — no SysV message queues
semget, semop — no SysV semaphores (futex is sufficient)
io_uring_* — not used by the Go stdlib net/file stack
nfsservctl, quotactl — no NFS or quota operations
kexec_load — no kernel replacement
name_to_handle_at, open_by_handle_at — no NFS handle ops
landlock_create_ruleset, landlock_add_rule, landlock_restrict_self —
Landlock self-restriction is a future Linux-gated enhancement (Linux 5.13+);
it would confine the daemon's filesystem view to the three writable mounts.
This requires a Go source change and must loud-degrade on older kernels;
it is deferred per the roadmap note under T2-15 (optional, not implemented).

syscalls._daemon_need: Go runtime init; clean process exit
Go runtime heap, stack growth, goroutine stacks, mmap-backed IO buffers
Go M:N scheduler — goroutines, OS threads, futex-based mutexes/channels
Go runtime TLS pointer setup on each OS thread
Go netpoller (epoll) for all socket and file I/O; eventfd for goroutine wakeup
fd lifecycle, CLOEXEC setup, dup for the flock guard, fcntl on the lock file

syscalls._group: Sockets: south face + ops listener + sd_notify
_daemon_need: socket(AF_UNIX, SOCK_STREAM) — create the per-session listen socket (south face)
socket(AF_INET, SOCK_STREAM) — ops listener loopback TCP (127.0.0.1:9464)
bind, listen — bind to the socket path in -south-socket-dir
accept / accept4 — accept guest connections on the south face
connect — health-check self-probe dials its own ops listener; sd_notify connect
getsockopt(SO_PEERCRED) — kernel-attested peer uid/pid gate (NFR-SEC-76)
setsockopt — configure socket options (REUSEADDR etc.)
getsockname / getpeername — local/remote address queries
recvmsg / sendmsg — framed messages over session socket AND sd_notify datagram
recvfrom / sendto — used by Go net package internally
shutdown — half-close on session teardown
socketpair — Go internal; used for self-pipe trick
open/openat/openat2 — open engine-root files, audit sink, socket dir
read/readv/pread64 — read customer files, audit chain scan at startup
write/writev/pwrite64 — write customer files, append audit records
lseek — seek within files during copy and hash-chain scan
readahead — Go IO optimisation
stat/lstat/fstat/statx — check file existence, size, mode, ownership
getdents64 — directory listing (List verb, TeardownScope recursive removal)
mkdir/mkdirat — create scope directory and staging area (.ocu-staging/)
rename/renameat/renameat2 — atomic stage-commit (WriteFile promotion)
link/linkat — hard-link in staging
unlink/unlinkat — delete files and stale socket on teardown
rmdir — remove empty scope/staging directories
chmod/fchmod/fchmodat — 0700 on socket dir after creation
chown/lchown/fchownat — ownership of created dirs (chown to 65532:65532)
utimensat — preserve mtime on rename/link
truncate/ftruncate — WriteFile create-then-truncate in staging
fsync — flush audit records to disk before acknowledging (NFR-SEC-79 fail-closed)
fdatasync — flush file data without metadata for staged writes
sync_file_range — optional IO hint used by Go on Linux
flock — LOCK_EX|LOCK_NB on the single-instance lock file (T2-7 guard);
prevents concurrent daemon instances from interleaving audit-chain writes

syscalls._daemon_need: Timestamps for audit records and log lines; crypto/rand for session ids

_daemon_need: signal.NotifyContext(SIGTERM, SIGINT) for graceful shutdown (T1-9);
rt_sigaction/rt_sigprocmask — register/unregister signal handlers
rt_sigreturn — return from signal handler
kill(getpid(), sig) — self-signal after first signal releases intercept
signalfd / signalfd4 — Go runtime uses signalfd on Linux
tgkill — Go runtime sends signals between OS threads

syscalls._daemon_need: uid/gid checks; SO_PEERCRED comparison; startup log lines

_daemon_need: uname — Go runtime queries Linux version for feature detection
getrlimit/prlimit64 — ceiling checks (-fd-ceiling, -in-flight-bytes)
sysinfo — Go runtime memory stats
fstatfs — runc/containerd container setup; Go os.Root API on some paths
membarrier — Go 1.19+ uses membarrier for GC write barriers on SMP
rseq — restartable sequences, used by newer glibc / Go runtime on Linux
sched_setaffinity — Go runtime may pin goroutines to CPUs
capget — Go os.exec checks capabilities before fork on some paths
